pending.body: Four "resume from failed run" tests in `tests/unit/workflow_engine_test.rs` are broken by the retry limit increase. The tests `resume_from_failed_run_skips_completed_stages` (line 3027), `resume_from_failed_docs_change_run_skips_completed_stages` (line 1033), `resume_from_failed_ci_improvement_run_skips_completed_stages` (line 1101), and `resume_from_failed_quick_dev_run_skips_completed_stages` (line 1407) all use `with_transient_failure(stage, 3)` but `TransportFailure` max_attempts is now 5. The 4th attempt succeeds, so `assert!(first_result.is_err())` fails. Fix: bump the transient failure count from 3 to 5 in each of these tests so the run exhausts retries and fails as expected.

The same four broken "resume from failed run" tests also lack `start_paused = true` on their `#[tokio::test]` attribute. With the new exponential backoff, each test incurs ~35 seconds of real-time delay (5s + 10s + 20s between retries). All four tests need `#[tokio::test(start_paused = true)]` to avoid real wall-clock backoff delays in CI.

src/adapters/process_backend.rs:486-499 and 1121-1132 still report a missing CLI as AppError::BackendUnavailable, and src/contexts/agent_execution/service.rs:166-169 plus 362-372 preserve/remap that to TransportFailure; src/shared/error.rs:300-305 then keeps BackendUnavailable retryable. In practice, a normal missing `claude`/`codex` binary still burns all 5 transport retries, so the new BinaryNotFound rule only applies to rarer spawn/exit-code paths (src/adapters/process_backend.rs:935-944, 1381-1384) and the fast-fail acceptance criterion is not actually met. Carry BinaryNotFound through the availability/build-command path as well.

src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:4619-4639 adds a plain tokio::time::sleep(backoff) before retrying, but that sleep never watches the cancellation token. If cancellation arrives after will_retry is computed (for example right after stage_failed is logged), the run now sits idle for 5-60 seconds before the next loop notices cancellation. Make the backoff wait cancellable with tokio::select! or an equivalent re-check.

Timeouts currently take the generic cleanup path instead of the failure-preserving one. The new timeout branch in src/adapters/process_backend.rs:987-1001 returns an error directly from spawn_and_wait(), and invoke() handles every such error with prepared.cleanup() at src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1150-1163. For Codex/OpenRouter, cleanup() deletes the schema/last-message temp files (src/adapters/process_backend.rs:55-63) rather than moving them into runtime/failed like cleanup_failed_invocation() does (src/adapters/process_backend.rs:66-90). A hung backend therefore becomes less inspectable than an ordinary non-zero exit. Route timeout failures through a preserving cleanup path.

run_snapshot.status_summary: running: final review restart round 2 -> Plan and Implement
